testcase.name: test_get_cert

failure.message: ssl.SSLCertVerificationError: [SSL: CERTIFICATE_VERIFY_FAILED] certificate verify failed: unable to get local issuer certificate (_ssl.c:1006)

testcase.failure:
def test_get_cert(): > assert Chepy("google.com").get_ssl_cert().o["subject"]["commonName"] != "" ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
tests/test_networking.py:52: _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ .venv/lib/python3.11/site-packages/decorator.py:235: in fun return caller(func, *(extras + args), **kw) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ chepy/core.py:52: in call_stack return func(*args, **kwargs) # lgtm [py/call-to-non-callable] ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ chepy/modules/networking.py:186: in get_ssl_cert with context.wrap_socket(sock, server_hostname=domain) as sslsock: ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ /Library/Frameworks/Python.framework/Versions/3.11/lib/python3.11/ssl.py:517: in wrap_socket return self.sslsocket_class._create( /Library/Frameworks/Python.framework/Versions/3.11/lib/python3.11/ssl.py:1108: in _create self.do_handshake() _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _
self = <ssl.SSLSocket [closed] fd=-1, family=2, type=1, proto=0>, block = False
@_sslcopydoc def do_handshake(self, block=False): self._check_connected() timeout = self.gettimeout() try: if timeout == 0.0 and block: self.settimeout(None) > self._sslobj.do_handshake() E ssl.SSLCertVerificationError: [SSL: CERTIFICATE_VERIFY_FAILED] certificate verify failed: unable to get local issuer certificate (_ssl.c:1006)
/Library/Frameworks/Python.framework/Versions/3.11/lib/python3.11/ssl.py:1379: SSLCertVerificationError

testcase.failure:
